title: 11 Herbal Essential Oils For Cystitis
intro_desc: If you're experiencing the discomfort of cystitis, you might be looking for a natural way to find some relief. Herbal teas have been used for centuries to help soothe the urinary tract and reduce inflammation. One example is Equisetum arvense, also known as horsetail, which is rich in silica and has anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease the pain and discomfort of cystitis. Another herb that may be beneficial is Melissa officinalis, or lemon balm, which has a calming effect on the body and can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate cystitis symptoms. Berberis vulgaris, or barberry, is also a good example, as it has been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections and may help reduce inflammation and ease pain. These herbs can be consumed as a tea by infusing them in hot water, and their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties can help bring relief to those suffering from cystitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense essential oils contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds that give it anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with cystitis. The alkaloids present in the oil, such as palustrine and fumaric acid, have a direct impact on the bacterial growth that causes cystitis. The antimicrobial properties of Equisetum arvense essential oils help to combat bacterial infections and reduce the risk of complications. The anti-inflammatory properties of the flavonoids and phenolic compounds in the oil also help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with cystitis.
remedies.image_alt: Equisetum arvense herbal essential oils for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Equisetum arvense plants, leaves and stems.
Wash the plants with 2 cups of water to remove dirt and impurities.
Use a steamer or a double boiler to heat 2 cups of water. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat.
Add the washed Equisetum arvense plants to the steamer or double boiler. Steam the plants for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Collect the essential oil by pouring the steamed plant mixture into a bowl. Let it sit for 2 hours, then carefully pour the oil from the top layer into a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils contains compounds like linalool, limonene, and geraniol, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause cystitis. The antimicrobial action of linalool, in particular, targets E. coli, a common bacteria linked to urinary tract infections. Melissa officinalis essential oils also exhibits antioxidant properties, which help to protect the body from oxidative stress caused by infection. Its anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the urinary tract, providing relief from the discomfort and pain associated with cystitis.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal essential oils for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 250ml of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers. Clean them with water.
Place the Melissa leaves and flowers in a clean glass container. Add 500ml of water.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler on low heat for 2-3 hours. Monitor the temperature at 60-70°C.
After heating, let the mixture cool and then strain it through a cheesecloth into another container.
Store the strained liquid in a dark glass bottle. For cystitis relief, use 5-7 drops of the essential oil in a bath or mix with a carrier oil for topical use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Berberis vulgaris essential oils contains berberine, a bioactive constituent that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Berberine helps to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to cystitis symptoms. It also has antiseptic properties, which can help to combat bacterial infections that often cause cystitis. Additionally, berberine has been shown to have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, which can help to reduce irritation and discomfort associated with cystitis. By reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and soothing the mucous membranes, Berberis vulgaris essential oils can help to alleviate cystitis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Berberis vulgaris herbal essential oils for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Berberis vulgaris roots and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil)
Dry the Berberis vulgaris roots in a warm, dry place for 1 week
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle
Mix 1/4 cup of the ground roots with 1 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, then strain and discard the solids

remedies.remedy_desc: Juniperus communis essential oils contains compounds like alpha-pinene, beta-pinene, and bornyl acetate. These constituents have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the urinary tract. The oil's diuretic properties also aid in flushing out bacteria and toxins from the body, making it easier to treat cystitis. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Juniperus communis essential oils help combat the growth of bacteria that can cause cystitis, such as E. coli. By reducing inflammation, fighting bacteria, and promoting urination, Juniperus communis essential oils can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
remedies.image_alt: Juniperus communis herbal essential oils for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Juniperus communis berries and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours, then let it cool down.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl.
Discard the solids and add 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or jojoba oil) to the liquid.
Store the Juniperus communis essential oil in a dark glass bottle and use 5-7 drops as needed to help alleviate cystitis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, liquiritin, and glabrene, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties help reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections in the urinary tract, which can cause cystitis. The antiseptic properties of glycyrrhizin and liquiritin also help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause cystitis. The essential oil's ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections makes it a potential natural remedy for cystitis. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 2 cups of water in a large pot.
Boil the water and add the dried roots. Reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Discard the solids.
Combine 1 cup of the strained liquid with 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) in a clean glass bottle.
Let the mixture sit for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the bottle daily. Strain and use the essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis essential oils contains a compound called quinine, which helps to fight bacterial infections that cause cystitis. The anti-inflammatory properties of Cinchona officinalis, specifically its alkaloids such as quinine and quinidine, reduce swelling and pain in the urinary tract. The antimicrobial properties of quinidine in Cinchona officinalis essential oils inhibit the growth of bacteria, including E. coli, which is a common cause of cystitis. The antiseptic properties of Cinchona officinalis essential oils also help to prevent infection and promote healing in the urinary tract. By reducing inflammation and inhibiting bacterial growth, Cinchona officinalis essential oils can help to alleviate symptoms of cystitis.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal essential oils for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Cinchona officinalis leaves, a glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil, and a cheesecloth.
Clean and dry the Cinchona officinalis leaves, then add them to the glass jar.
Pour 1 cup of carrier oil over the leaves and close the jar. Store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container. Discard the solids and keep the liquid.
Transfer the liquid to a small bottle and use 5-7 drops in a warm bath or mix with 1 tablespoon of carrier oil for relief from cystitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium macrocarpon essential oils contains boswellic acids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that make it beneficial for cystitis. The boswellic acids have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the urinary tract. The flavonoids, particularly quercetin, have antioxidant properties that can help protect the bladder and urinary tract from damage. The phenolic acids, such as gallic acid, have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections that can cause cystitis. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of Vaccinium macrocarpon essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of cystitis and promote healing in the urinary tract.
remedies.image_alt: Vaccinium macrocarpon herbal essential oils for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Vaccinium macrocarpon berries (also known as cranberries) and a clean glass jar.
Combine 1 cup of berries with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into the glass jar.
Add 1 tablespoon of carrier oil (such as coconut or olive oil) to the jar and mix well.
Store the essential oil in a cool, dark place and use 5-7 drops per bath for cystitis relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains bioactive constituents like turmerone, atlantone, and zingiberene. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and fight off infections in the urinary tract. Turmerone and atlantone are specifically effective against bacterial growth, which can cause cystitis. Zingiberene also has potent antioxidant properties, which help protect the bladder from oxidative damage and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Curcuma longa essential oils can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis and promote a speedy recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried Curcuma longa rhizomes and 4 cups of carrier oil (like coconut oil).
Chop the Curcuma longa into small pieces and add them to a clean glass jar.
Pour the carrier oil over the chopped Curcuma longa, making sure they are completely covered.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily to help the oil infuse.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container. Discard the solids and use the oil as needed for cystitis relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra essential oils contains isoflavones and flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and pain in the urinary tract, making Sambucus nigra essential oils beneficial for cystitis. The flavonoids in Sambucus nigra essential oils also have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the bladder from oxidative stress. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Sambucus nigra essential oils may also help to reduce the frequency and severity of cystitis symptoms. Sambucus nigra essential oils has been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections and may help to soothe and calm the bladder.
remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal essential oils for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Sambucus nigra flowers, a clean glass jar, and a carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil.
Fill the jar with the Sambucus nigra flowers and cover them with 2 cups of carrier oil.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Discard the solids and store the Sambucus nigra essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale essential oils contains a compound called flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with cystitis. It also contains sesquiterpenes and diterpenes, which have antimicrobial properties that can inhibit the growth of bacteria causing the infection. The anti-inflammatory properties of flavonoids in Taraxacum officinale essential oils can also help soothe the bladder and reduce pain. Additionally, the diuretic properties of the essential oil can help increase urine production, which can help flush out the bacteria causing the infection. The antimicrobial properties of sesquiterpenes and diterpenes can also help prevent the spread of the infection.
remedies.image_alt: Taraxacum officinale herbal essential oils for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Taraxacum officinale flowers and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or jojoba oil)
Heat 1/2 cup of water in a saucepan over low heat. Place a heat-resistant glass bowl over the saucepan.
Add 1/4 cup of fresh Taraxacum officinale flowers to the glass bowl and pour 1/2 cup of carrier oil over them.
Steep the flowers in the oil for 2-3 hours or overnight, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter.
Discard the solids and store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle, using 5-7 drops per dose for cystitis relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica essential oils contains a compound called histamine, which acts as a natural diuretic to help increase urine production and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract. The oil also contains flavonoids, specifically quercetin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the bladder and reduce symptoms of cystitis. Other bioactive constituents, such as caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid, have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to cystitis. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of Urtica dioica essential oils help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with cystitis, promoting healing and reducing the risk of recurrent infections. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Urtica dioica essential oils can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis and support overall urinary tract health.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal essential oils for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves and flowers. Wear gloves to protect your skin from the plant's urticating hairs.
Use a clean glass jar to add 2 cups of water. Add 1/4 cup of witch hazel (optional).
Combine the Urtica dioica leaves and flowers with the water mixture in a blender or food processor. Blend until the plant material is well broken down.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and let it sit for 2-3 hours to allow the plant's oils to infuse into the water.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle. Discard the solids and store the Urtica dioica essential oil in a dark glass bottle.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent cystitis. Certain herbs like uva ursi and juniper berries have antibacterial properties that can help fight off infections. Some herbal teas also have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the urinary tract and reduce irritation. Drinking herbal tea regularly may help keep cystitis at bay, but it's not a guarantee.
faq_safe: You can safely drink herbal teas for cystitis every day, but it's best to choose teas that are gentle on your bladder. Some examples include peppermint, chamomile, and corn silk tea. These teas may help soothe your bladder and reduce discomfort. However, if you're sensitive to caffeine or other ingredients, start with small amounts to see how your body reacts.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help soothe cystitis symptoms, but it may take a few days to notice results. Some people find relief within 48-72 hours, while others may need a week or more to feel better. Drinking a cup of herbal tea 2-3 times a day can help calm the bladder and reduce discomfort.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for cystitis is best done in the morning. This allows the tea to work throughout the day, helping to reduce symptoms and prevent future infections. Some herbal teas, like uva ursi and juniper berries, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can aid in healing the bladder lining.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for cystitis. Some herbs, like goldenseal and saw palmetto, may slow down the body's removal of certain medications, making them more potent. This can be a problem, especially if you're taking antibiotics. It's essential to be cautious and talk to your doctor about any herbal teas you're using.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some can cause problems, like uterine contractions or low birth weight. Cystitis, or bladder infection, might tempt you to try herbal teas for relief, but it's not a good idea. These teas might interact with other pregnancy symptoms or affect your baby's health.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a good option for cystitis relief while breastfeeding. However, some herbs can pass into breast milk and affect the baby. For example, pennyroyal and sage can be too strong. Choose teas like chamomile or peppermint, which are gentle and usually safe for both you and your baby.
title_2: 11 Essential Oils for Diabetics with Cystitis
